objective: Map JOC, Opus, Echo Forge, Victus, and active ION UI canon sources into a candidate UI/frontend excellence domain.

active_ion_sources.finding: ION work surfaces must preserve pressure, synthesis, evidence, recognition, formalization, receipt, and continuation instead of only rendering static pages.
JOC-class UI requires top bar, left rail, main work surface, right inspector, and bottom timeline/proof surfaces, with receipt, route, visual evidence, repair, automation, and compute lenses.

Cockpit is a projection organ, not a dashboard; named components include top bar, rails, main work surface, inspector, timeline, receipt rail, route panel, context graph, repair queue, and browser automation overlay.
Prior local UI failure was monolithic; the correct shell splits top/page navigation, icon rails, drawers, main chat/work surface, right inspector/chat, and bottom timeline.
Existing generic UI/UX domain covers ordinary design, state, accessibility, and polish but does not yet encode JOC/Opus/Echo/Victus work-surface law.

external_read_only_sources.finding: JOC is a central nervous system, not a browser, IDE, or app launcher; layout canon uses fixed top bar, left rail, main content, optional right panel, and optional bottom bar.
Opus canon defines lucid engine drawers: icon bars, full/top/bottom split-click drawer modes, sub-tabs inside drawers, and configurable drawer widths to prevent scrolling monoliths.

Master vision frames JOC as a mission dashboard and AI orchestration center, not a browser or launcher. Primary pillars include mission dashboard, session manager, dispatch engine, results synthesizer, and project catalog.

Architecture canon defines presentation, orchestration, and infrastructure tiers; AI drivers expose session state, login health, prompt injection, response extraction, quota, conversation, memory, and health-check contracts.

Compute/IDE canon expands JOC into a three-ring compute fabric and IDE-grade shell: activity bar, tabs, split panes, command palette, status bar, bottom output panel, resource monitor, and documented stop controls.

Roadmap canon identifies the signature session page: live browser viewport plus automation overlay, injection/extraction highlights, DOM health, communication pipeline, debug rail, and session health monitoring.
DXL style requires matte-black instrument-panel density, small monospace labels, strict borders, no emoji, no Material colors, no rounded corners over 2px, and screenshot validation.

V3 Intelligence Map proposes algorithmic certainty UI: instant dictionary/index search, AST/structural blueprint view, dependency web, reactive OS stream, and command palette that visibly highlights selected context.

Materiality canon explores high-end skeuomorphic/PBR DOM techniques: unified light source, gradients as material, shadows as depth, SVG filter material grain, dynamic light uniforms, caustics, and physics-based motion. Use for selected tactile controls, not as a base-shell default.
JOC implementation pattern used React/Vite/TypeScript/Zustand and a hierarchy of root layout, icon bar, left drawer, main workspace, and inspector panel.
UI agent is a signal-driven renderer that transforms typed render requests into pixels; it must not own analysis, state, or command authority.
Echo Forge contributes a streaming cognition/event timeline: memory, plan, execute, verify, retry, audit, synthesize, reflect, evolve, and complete.
Victus contributes typed, portable, provenance-backed file/state thinking and the loop contextualize, reflect, plan, gate, execute, audit, deliver.
Victus failure evidence warns against overwritten monolithic state; UI state and agent/domain movement require provenance, history, and receipts.

derived_domain_need.reason: Advanced ION apps need a UI domain that fuses JOC shell law, Opus drawer architecture, AI-driver/session/mission surfaces, compute/IDE cockpit layout, Echo Forge event timelines, Victus provenance, material-control craft, and active ION maintained-work-surface governance.
derived_domain_need.relationship: higher-order specialist domain for complex frontend/work-surface applications

non_claims: External sources were read as evidence, not accepted current law.
No production frontend was changed by this evidence map.
This map does not promote the new domain into accepted ION canon.
Some backup/archive areas may contain additional inaccessible or redundant evidence.
